task_completion.description: Added at least four new partition walls and five interior doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view in Sweet Home 3D. Identify at least four newly drawn wall segments (thick lines) and five door symbols (arcs indicating door swings) placed within the layout.

task_completion.description: Created at least five named functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view for room labels (text on the floor area) showing at least five distinct named zones matching the required functional areas (e.g., Reception, Relaxation Lounge, Treatment Rooms, Locker/Wet Rooms).

task_completion.description: Applied distinct floor colors or textures to at least three rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 3D view or the colored 2D plan. Check that at least three distinct room areas have custom floor colors or textures applied, visually separating wet and dry areas.

task_completion.description: Furnished Treatment Rooms and Locker/Wet Rooms with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view. Count at least 3 beds (massage tables), 3 storage units/cabinets, and 3 chairs/stools in treatment areas. Count at least 4 wardrobes (lockers), 2 shower cabins/trays, and 2 toilets or sinks in the locker/wet areas.

task_completion.description: Furnished the Reception and Lounge area with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view in the reception/lounge area. Identify at least 1 desk, 3 comfortable chairs/sofas (lounge seats), and 2 indoor plants.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to create the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window open with the layout being actively edited, rather than a terminal window running a script to generate the .sh3d file.

integrity.description: Furniture and architectural elements were placed using the built-in catalog.
integrity.visual_evidence: The catalog pane on the left side of the Sweet Home 3D interface should be visible, and the items in the plan should correspond to standard Sweet Home 3D catalog items.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file to the correct path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar should display 'spa_layout.sh3d' or a save dialog should be visible pointing to /home/ga/Documents/spa_layout.sh3d.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 100 points distributed across 5 categories (20 points each). The starter file 'spa_wellness_starter.sh3d' is an empty shell with no furniture. The VLM must verify: 1) >=4 new walls and >=5 doors. 2) >=5 named rooms (Reception, Relaxation Lounge, Treatment Rooms, Locker/Wet Rooms). 3) >=3 rooms with custom floor colors/textures. 4) Treatment/Locker furniture: >=3 beds, >=3 storage units, >=3 therapist seats, >=4 wardrobes (lockers), >=2 showers, >=2 toilets or sinks. 5) Reception/Lounge furniture: >=1 desk, >=3 lounge seats, >=2 decorative plants. Total specific furniture items should be at least 23.
